titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the African American Film Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday March 20, 2021 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from various sources, 2012-2019. For more information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into twelve groupings: February 2012 Acquisition; March 2013 Acquisition; 15 October 2013 Acquisition [1 of 2]; 15 October 2013 Acquisition [2 of 2]; December 2013 Acquisition; July 2015 Acquisition; 19 June 2017 Acquisition; January 2018 Acquisition; August 2018 Acquisition; January 2019 Acquisition; July 2019 Acquisition; and February 2021 Acquisition.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: African American Film Collection. James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The African American Film Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The African American Film Collection consists of scripts, photographs, and other materials related to the production and promotion of films and television shows created by, starring, or documenting the experience of African Americans.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional scripts, photographs, and other materials documenting the work or portrayal of African Americans in motion pictures and television acquired before the creation of this collection can be found in the Beinecke Library's collections. To find additional materials, search within Orbis or Archives at Yale.
Scripts for television programs featuring African American actors, entertainers, and topics can be found in African American Television Scripts (JWJ MSS 90).
Additional scripts can be found in the James Weldon Johnson Collection Manuscript Miscellany (JWJ MSS MISC). They include, but may not be limited to, the following:

list.item: Berg, Richard. "Shelia" screenplay. JWJ MSS MISC Group 55, F-1.
Connelly, Marc. "Cabin in the Sky" screenplay. JWJ MSS MISC Group 58, F-1.
Foreman, Carl. "High Noon" screenplay. JWJ MSS MIsc Group 61, F-1.
Frost, Lee. "The Man with Two Heads" screenplay. JWJ MSS Misc Group 64, F-1.
Julien, Max. "Cleopatra Jones" screenplay. JWJ MSS Misc Group 56, F-1.
McCloy, Terrence. "Lady Sings the Blues: The Billie Holiday Story" screenplay. JWJ MSS MISC Group 69, F-1.
Nash, N. Richard. "Porgy and Bess" screenplay. JWJ MSS MISC Group 54, F-1.
Schumacher, Joel. "Capitol Cab" screenplay. JWJ MSS MISC Group 57, F-1.
Silliphant, Stirling. "Shaft in Africa" screenplay. JWJ MSS MISC Group 65, F-1.
Vidor, King. "Hallelujah!" screenplay. JWJ MSS MISC Group 60, F-1.
Weller, Michael. "Ragtime" screenplay. JWJ MSS MISC Group 66, F-1.

relatedmaterial.p: Select related materials cataloged individually include the following:

list.item: Coen, Franklin. "Black gunn" screenplay. YCAL MSS 478

subject.part: African Americans in the motion picture industry

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012.

did.unittitle: Photographs from "Sugar Hill Baby," "The Peanut Man," "Native Son," and "Bright Road"

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from the William Reese Co. on the James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection Fund, 2013.

acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013.

"I Crossed The Color Line" promotional photographs

did.unittitle: "Sanford and Son" promotional photographs with typescript captions

"Good Times" promotional photographs with typescript captions
"Flip Wilson" promotional photographs with typescript captions
"That's My Mama" promotional photographs with typescript captiona
"Wattstax" promotional photographs with typescript and holograph captions
"The Autobiography of Miss Jane Pittman" promotional photographs with typescript captions
"The Education of Sonny Carson" promotional photographs
"The Legend of Nigger Charley" promotional photographs
"Slaughter's Big Rip-Off" promotional photographs with typescript and holograph captions
"The Soul of Nigger Charley" promotional photographs with typescript captions
"Soul Soldier" ["The Red, White, and Black"] promotional photographs
"The Spook Who Sat By The Door" promotional photographs
"If He Hollers Let Him Go" promotional photographs

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

did.unittitle: "The Legend of Frankie and Johnny" screenplay
"How Stella Got Her Groove Back" screenplay
"The Swinging Story of Johnny Melody" screenplay
Una Joven Llamada Joanna" promotional photograph
"Scent of Mystery" promotional photograph with "Their Shining Hour" caption
Untitled promotional photograph with holograph notes on back
"Princess Tam Tam" promotional photographs with holograph notes on back
Photograph of Joanna Shimkus and others with holograph notes on back

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

did.unittitle: "The Education of Sonny Carson" partial screenplay, shooting schedule, research, and related correspondence
"The Sign in Sidney Brustein's Window" screenplay belonging to Ken Kercheval with holograph annotations

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2018.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

did.unittitle: "Penitentiary III" third draft screenplay with revision pages
"Come Back, Charleston Blue" revised final draft screenplay belonging to Ron Munkasci
"Dr. Black, Mr. Hyde" screenplay , production materials, and treatment draft
"The Jesse Owens Story" final draft screenplay

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Walter Reuben, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

did.unittitle: "Mandingo" screenplay, holograph notes, price lists, and related correspondence
